entities.name: Logan
entities.context: Collaborator who reframes Careerspan's value as compounding passive opportunities over time

entities.name: Ilya
entities.context: Collaborator who raises classic SaaS conundrums around employer needs and technical constraints

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; a platform that matches candidates to roles with compounding leverage from each interaction

entities.name: Ongoing Leverage
entities.context: UX promise that each additional time investment by a candidate increases Careerspan's ability to apply on their behalf to future roles

entities.name: Compounding Passive Opportunities
entities.context: Logan's reframe: stories and interactions accumulate into more opportunities surfaced over time, not just one-off applications

entities.name: Volume vs. Curation Tension
entities.context: SaaS conundrum where some employers want 500 applicants while others want a curated five

entities.name: User Environment Constraints
entities.context: Technical reality that browser health and outdated setups can break well-designed product flows

relationships.context: Reframes Careerspan's value prop as each story and interaction compounding into passive opportunities
Contributes strategic framing to Careerspan's value narrative
Raises the classic SaaS dilemma of serving both volume-seekers and curation-seekers
Highlights that Careerspan is constrained by users' browser health and outdated setups
Provides SaaS strategy perspective on product constraints
Core UX promise: every additional candidate investment enables future one-click applications
Two framings of the same core idea—time invested now yields increasing returns later
Careerspan tries to cut through volume noise but must serve employers who still optimize for scale
